intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: A health dashboard with warm earth tones (terracotta, sage, cream) and soft shadows will feel more nurturing and personal than cool or clinical palettes
facade.label: Warm, soft colors
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward intimate, approachable interfaces—avoid sterile medical aesthetics; lean into natural, lived-in feeling
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer clarity through contrast and brightness; cool tones, high saturation, or minimal warmth might resonate better for this use case

facade.hypothesis: A persistent left sidebar for experiment navigation paired with spacious card layouts reduces cognitive load and makes health data feel less clinical
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure (sidebar vs tabs) × Information density (dense vs spacious)
facade.label: Sidebar + cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values persistent context and breathing room; likely favors exploration over density; sidebar becomes core wayfinding
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer horizontal navigation or denser packing; wants more data per screen; sidebar feels like wasted vertical space

evidence.hypothesis: A health dashboard with warm earth tones (terracotta, sage, cream) and soft shadows will feel more nurturing and personal than cool or clinical palettes
evidence.implication: User gravitates toward intimate, approachable interfaces—avoid sterile medical aesthetics; lean into natural, lived-in feeling

facade.hypothesis: A health dashboard with gentle, curved UI elements will feel more approachable and less clinical than sharp geometries
facade.axisTargeted: Roundness vs sharpness — emotional warmth in form
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward softer, more humanized interfaces; likely prefers calming aesthetics over clinical precision
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer clean, modern minimalism or technical clarity; less drawn to tactile softness in design

facade.hypothesis: Users building personal health trackers prefer warm, encouraging language over clinical jargon—making the interface feel like a supportive coach rather than a medical device.
facade.label: Conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: Adopt casual, motivational voice throughout (e.g., 'Nice work logging today!' vs 'Data entry recorded'). Use contractions, direct address, gentle humor.
facade.rejectImplies: Lean into clinical precision and formal language. Use passive voice, medical terminology, structured professional tone. Position as a serious health tool, not a lifestyle companion.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer animated transitions that ease data in/out rather than instant appearance, making the dashboard feel continuous and less jarring
facade.label: Smooth slide-ins
facade.acceptImplies: Prioritize fluid micro-interactions: data cards slide in on load, metric changes animate smoothly, modal overlays fade rather than pop
facade.rejectImplies: Users want speed and clarity over motion; go snappy and instant—cards appear immediately, switches toggle with no delay, minimal animation overhead

facade.hypothesis: Users want to see their most important health metric (e.g., weight, sleep hours) dominate the screen with just one sentence of context, rather than a narrative breakdown or full data table.
facade.axisTargeted: dense data vs key metrics — how much numbers vs narrative does the dashboard lean on
facade.label: Big number, small story
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers metric-first design: prominent KPIs with minimal explanation. Dashboard should emphasize scale and individual numbers over trends or patterns.
facade.rejectImplies: User wants more context around the numbers: trends, comparisons, or interpretive text. Numbers alone feel hollow without the story of what they mean.

facade.hypothesis: Subtle animations on data inputs and experiment logging (like smooth transitions when toggling metrics or confirming entries) will make the dashboard feel responsive and alive without introducing clinical coldness or overwhelming visual noise
facade.axisTargeted: Organic vs digital — whether movement and polish feel natural/warm or mechanical/sterile
facade.label: Animated micro-interactions
facade.acceptImplies: User wants the interface to feel tactile and responsive; small moments of delight build emotional connection to health tracking; avoid static, lifeless dashboards
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers a calm, unchanging interface; animation—even subtle—reads as unnecessary or distracting; the dashboard should recede and let data speak quietly
